items.description: One of the best aquariums in the US. Open early to beat cruise crowds. Shark tunnel + sea turtles. ~90 min

items.title: Turtle Town snorkel
items.description:
Makena Landing (better snorkling) or Maluaka Beach (more family friendly). Take turns watching W while the other person swims. Either we buy equipment or rent it night before. 

Ocean is most ocean in morning so should aim to go around 8 AM.

items.title: Afternoon @ Beach or Pool

items.title: ʻAhihi-Kīnaʻu tide pools
items.description:
Protected marine reserve, lava coastline. Morning low tide should be 9:30-10:30 AM.

Spend 10–15 minutes beforehand reading about the reserve's common species (humuhumunukunukuāpuaʻa, surgeonfish, sea urchins, hermit crabs, etc.), then turn it into a scavenger hunt.

Check for conservation closures. Backups: Ulua Beach, Makena Landing

•Go in the morning before the lava gets hot.
•Closed-toe shoes or sturdy sandals help; the lava is sharp.
•Carry more water than you think you'll need.

items.title: Sunset at Keawakapu Beach
items.description: One of the best sunset beaches. 25 minute walk with Wren in stroller.

items.title: Drive Upcountry
items.description:
Morning drive into Upcountry through Kula and Makawao — cooler air, rolling hills, and farm country. 

Stop at Surfing Goat Dairy for a quick visit and cheese tasting

Continue through Makawao, a historic paniolo (Hawaiian cowboy) town with small shops and bakeries.

items.description:
Look for signs that say Limousine Bus or Kyoto. Can tap in w/ Suica card.

Ride to Kyoto Station Hachijo-guchi
50-55 minutes typical, 75 minutes if traffic is bad. Bus is comfortable, has seatbelts, AC, and is reliably quiet. Wren can sleep. Bus drops you on the south side of Kyoto Station (Hachijo-guchi exit).

105 Machigashira-Cho, Sanjo-Agaru, Shinmachi-Dori, Nakagyo-Ku, Kyoto

items.title: Nishiki Market grocery run
items.description: Nishiki Market is a 5 minute walk, perfect for a grocery run and light grazing on tofu, pickles, and street snacks to stock the apartment. Easy first evening, early night, let Wren decompress from the travel day.

items.title: Higashiyama: Ninenzaka, Sannenzaka, Kiyomizudera
items.description: Morning walk through the preserved stone-paved lanes of Ninenzaka and Sannenzaka — some of the most photogenic streetscapes in Japan, lined with traditional wooden shops. Continue up to Kiyomizudera, the famous temple built into the hillside with a sweeping wooden stage jutting out over the valley. Back to the apartment by midday for Wren's nap and heat break. Carrier

items.title: Gion at dusk + Pontocho dinner
items.description: Walk Gion at dusk when the light is soft — Hanamikoji Street, paper lanterns, the chance of spotting a geiko or maiko heading to an engagement. Dinner in Pontocho alley, wander until you find a skewer spot you like.

items.title: Fushimi Inari (early)
items.description: Aim for gates by 8 AM, before heat and crowds arrive. The thousands of vermillion torii gates tunnel up the mountain and feel genuinely otherworldly in the morning mist. Walk as far as feels right with Wren — the lower section alone is spectacular and takes about 45 minutes, the full hike is 2-3 hours and unnecessary. Back at the apartment by 11am. Carrier. Afternoon completely free

items.title: Gion Matsuri Yoiyama (early evening)
items.description: Head out around 4:30-5:00 PM. The floats on the streets around Shinmachi are assembled and accessible up close — you can watch the hayashi musicians rehearsing on them, see the tapestries and carvings in detail without fighting nighttime crowds. Walk through the Byōbu Matsuri displays in the merchant homes nearby. Wren's in the carrier or SmartAngel, you're grazing from early food stalls, back upstairs by 6:30 for her bedtime. You get the substance of the festival without needing darkness.

items.title: Sagano Scenic Railway
items.description: Stroller day. Take the JR Sagano Line from Kyoto Station to Saga-Arashiyama (17 min, ¥240). Walk next door to Saga Torokko Station and board the 10:02 train to Kameoka. The ride is 25 minutes through the Hozu River gorge — forested cliffs, river below. Skip the open-air cars in July. (Wren goes in the carrier for this; stroller must be folded on board). At Kameoka Torokko, walk 5 minutes to JR Umahori Station and take the train back to Arashiyama (7 min, ¥200). You're back by 11:00 with the whole afternoon ahead.

items.title: Bamboo Grove + Tenryuji
items.description:
Stroller day. From Saga-Arashiyama station, walk into the bamboo grove — it's right there, and worth seeing even though it's more of a 10-minute photo walk than a destination. Continue through to Tenryuji, which is the real draw: the 14th-century garden with its pond reflecting the Arashiyama mountains behind it is one of the finest borrowed-scenery compositions in Japan. The paths are stroller-friendly. Lunch in Arashiyama after, plenty of casual spots. Then a slow walk along the Hozu River before heading back mid-afternoon. 

Sit on the left side heading to Kameoka for the river views. Skip Car 5 (the open-air "Rich" car) — no reserved seats, standing only, and exposed to July sun with a 13-month-old is a bad idea.

items.title: Gion Matsuri night - solo rotation
items.description: One of you out 8-9 PM, swap. Lanterns lit on floats, summer festival energy

items.title: Gion Matsuri Ato Matsuri procession
items.description: The procession of enormous yamaboko floats moves through central Kyoto starting around 9am — position yourselves early on the route. The floats are extraordinary, some dating back centuries, decorated with tapestries and pulled through the streets by teams of men. Done by early afternoon, then back to rest. Evening: the Yoiyama street stalls are still active around Gion — food vendors, yukata crowds, lanterns everywhere. One of the great summer festival atmospheres in Japan. Carrier.

items.title: Nara day trip
items.description:
JR Nara Line from Kyoto Station, about 45 minutes each way. Nara Park is flat, stroller friendly, deer roaming freely everywhere — fair warning, they bow for crackers and can be pushy about it at her height. Keep crackers hidden and approach older deer who are more patient.

Todai-ji temple houses one of the largest bronze Buddhas in the world and is worth seeing. Back to Kyoto by late afternoon.

items.title: Ship bags via Takkyubin to Tokyo MIMARU
items.description: Mimaru to Mimaru can be arranged at front desk. MIMARU Tokyo Ueno Inaricho  arriving July 29.

items.title: Shinkansen Kyoto to Odawara
items.description: Taxi to Kyoto Station, Shinkansen to Odawara (~2 hours). At Odawara, skip the Hakone Tozan railway — taxi directly to Hakone Ginyu (~20 minutes, ~¥5,000). Total door-to-door from MIMARU Shinmachi Sanjo to Ginyu is about 3 hours. Check in around 2pm, change into yukata, first soak in the private onsen.

items.title: Check in Hakone Ginyu
items.description:
Hakone-Ginyu
箱根吟遊
Address : 100-1 Miyanoshita, Hakonemachi,Ashigarashimogun, Kanagawaken 250-0404

Taxi directly from Odawara Station to Hakone Ginyu (~20 minutes, ~¥5,000). Buy the Hakone Freepass at the Odawara station machines before grabbing the taxi; you'll use it for exploring over the next two days, just not today. Check in around 2pm, change into yukata, private onsen, kaiseki dinner, decompress.

items.title: Long onsen + kaiseki dinner
items.description: Mid-afternoon return from the day, relax with private onsen soak and enjoy Kaiseki dinner.

items.title: Hakone Loop: shrine, lake, ropeway
items.description: Shrine, Lake, Ropeway Morning: Bus from Miyanoshita Station to Moto-Hakone (~19 min, hourly — check schedule night before). Hakone Shrine early while cool and quiet. Torii gate in the lake, cedar-lined approach. 5-10 min walk to pier → sightseeing boat across Lake Ashi to Togendai (~25 min). Ropeway from Togendai up through Owakudani — black eggs, sulfur vents, Fuji views if clear. Continue ropeway to Sounzan → cable car down to Gora → Tozan Railway back to Miyanoshita.

items.title: Long onsen + kaiseki dinner
items.description: Mid-afternoon return from the day, relax with private onsen soak and enjoy Kaiseki dinner.

items.title: Hakone Open-Air Museum
items.description: Tozan Railway one stop to Chokokunomori. 2-3 hours, Picasso pavilion, foot onsen.

items.title: Final onsen sessions + last kaiseki
items.description: One final onsen soak and dinner kaiseki

items.title: Check out and head to Tokyo
items.description: Departure. Breakfast at ryokan. Check out by 10:30. Taxi from Ginyu to Odawara Station (~20 minutes, ~¥5,000). Shinkansen to Tokyo Station (~35 minutes). JR to Ueno or taxi to MIMARU Ueno Inaricho (~15 minutes either way). Arriving early afternoon, bags should be waiting.

items.title: Ueno Park orientation walk
items.description: Easy walk into Ueno Park once everyone is settled — open paths, shaded areas, duck pond. No agenda beyond getting oriented.

items.title: Tsukiji Outer Market
items.description: Arrive hungry and move slowly (Thursday is the sweet spot to go). The focus is seafood: fresh uni, grilled scallops, tamagoyaki, and tuna, mostly eaten standing. Sit down at a small sushi counter for an early lunch (¥2,000–3,000). Carrier.

items.title: Ginza depachika browse
items.description: Spend an hour in a depachika basement at Ginza Six or Mitsukoshi — well-organized, air-conditioned, and easy to browse without crowds at midday. Japanese sweets, prepared foods, and high-quality produce. No rush in the afternoon

items.title: JINS Ueno - eyeglasses order
items.description: Start the day at JINS Ueno right when they open — eye exam and glasses order handled efficiently, usually within about an hour, with pickup later the same day or next morning.

items.title: Tokyo National Museum
items.description: Head straight into Tokyo National Museum right when it opens. Don’t try to cover everything — 60–90 minutes focused on the highlights is perfect. Prioritize the main Honkan (Japanese Gallery): samurai armor, swords, and ceramics. It’s air-conditioned, calm, and stroller-friendly, and it delivers a strong sense of history without dragging. Stroller

items.title: Yanaka neighborhood + Kayaba Coffee
items.description:
Decompress in Ueno Park with a loop around Shinobazu Pond — flat, shaded, and an easy reset walk. In August, the lotus leaves blanket the water even if the blooms close by mid-morning. Grab a coffee or snack from a kiosk and take it slow.

Late morning, walk about ten minutes into Yanaka — one of the few areas that still feels like old Tokyo. Follow the natural path through Yanaka Cemetery (wide, shaded, very peaceful), then drift into the surrounding small streets with shops and cafes.

Stop at Kayaba Coffee for thick toast and drip coffee as a late breakfast or early lunch.

items.title: Shinjuku Gyoen
items.description: Start the morning at Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden — one of the easiest large parks in Tokyo with a stroller. Wide paved paths, shaded walking loops, quiet garden sections, and open lawns make it ideal for a slower-paced morning with room for Wren to move around without the intensity of the city. Enter via the Shinjuku Gate for the smoothest access and flattest terrain. Aim to arrive early, before the late-morning heat and humidity settle in. Plan for roughly 90 minutes to 2 hours with a relaxed snack or drink break along the way. ¥500 entry. Stroller

items.title: Omotesando + Kurono Tokyo
items.description: Head toward Omotesando — Tokyo's most comfortable major walking boulevard with broad sidewalks, heavy tree cover, striking architecture, and frequent cafés or department stores to duck into for AC breaks. Eli slips away briefly to visit Kurono Tokyo's Aoyama salon (2-10-22 Kita-Aoyama; roughly 8–10 minutes from Omotesando Station toward Gaienmae, or 4 minutes from Gaienmae Station Exit 3). Quick 20-minute browse: display models, Grand Urushi Aoyama boutique-only editions, photos, then regroup.

items.title: Ura-Harajuku backstreets
items.description:
Continue into the Ura-Harajuku backstreets and Cat Street — lower-key than central Harajuku and much easier with a stroller. Small boutiques, tucked-away cafés, side alleys, and shaded breaks give the neighborhood a slower rhythm that works well in the afternoon heat.

Finish with a brief pass through Takeshita Street for the spectacle: loud music, crepes, crowds, fashion chaos, and pure teenage Tokyo energy. Keep expectations calibrated — it is narrow, congested, and not especially stroller-friendly, especially in summer. Twenty minutes is usually enough before slipping back out toward Meiji-dori.

Return to Ueno in the early evening. The cleanest one-seat ride is JR Yamanote from Harajuku Station direct to Ueno (~20 min, no transfer) — busy at rush hour but stroller-manageable on the platform. If the JR Harajuku crowds feel like too much, Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line from Meiji-jingumae is calmer at the origin but doesn't stop at Ueno itself; you'd exit at Yushima or Nezu (~10 min walk to the hotel area) or transfer at Otemachi. Pick based on whether you'd rather optimize for a quieter boarding or a transfer-free ride.

items.title: Eli watch day: Ginza or Nakano Broadway
items.description: Eli heads out independently — either Ginza boutiques or Nakano Broadway, depending on interest.

items.title: Tina + Wren: Nakameguro Canal
items.description: Tina and Wren walk along the Nakameguro Canal in the morning — flat, shaded, and stroller-friendly, with cafés spaced naturally along the route. Stroller

items.title: Daikanyama
items.description: In the afternoon, move into Daikanyama, a low-rise neighborhood with a relaxed pace and good pedestrian flow. Tsutaya Books is a practical anchor — air-conditioned, seating available, and easy to spend an hour without pressure. If more outdoor space is needed, Komazawa Olympic Park is a 15-minute taxi ride away, with wide lawns and family-friendly paths. Stroller

items.title: Kappabashi knife shopping
items.description: Head to Kappabashi as a family. The main street is flat and stroller-friendly, and the knife shops are calm, air-conditioned indoor spaces — a good, low-key start to the day. Keep browsing focused rather than exhaustive. Eli targets Mcusta/Moki folding knives (¥15,000–25,000; bring cash). Aim to arrive by ~10:00–10:15 AM, giving a solid 60–90 minutes before needing to move.

items.title: Wanariya shibori workshop
items.description:
~11:45 AM: Walk to Wanariya (~10 minutes from Kappabashi). Arrive a few minutes early for check-in.

12:00 PM: Wanariya shibori workshop — 90 minutes of indigo dyeing. Already registered, they know Wrennie is coming.

items.title: Sumida River water bus to Hamarikyu
items.description: Sumida River water bus from Asakusa to Hamarikyu (¥980/adult; Wren free; garden entry included). Stroller friendly on boat Relaxed boat ride, then a shaded garden stroll. Wrap up by ~4 PM.

items.title: Tokyo Sea Life Park
items.description: Visit Tokyo Sea Life Park in the morning. The aquarium is well laid out, with the large tuna tank as the centerpiece, and works well as a 1.5–2 hour visit. About 20 minutes from Ueno by train. Stroller.

items.title: Kasai Rinkai Park
items.description: Walk next door to Kasai Rinkai Park — wide waterfront paths, open space, and plenty of room to slow down. This area is popular with local families and doesn’t feel rushed.

items.title: Inokashira Park (Kichijoji)
items.description: Morning in Inokashira Park — ponds, open grass, a small zoo, and consistent shade. Stroller

items.title: Shimokitazawa wander
items.description:
Around midday, drift into Kichijoji for lunch. No agenda — find somewhere to sit, eat, let Wren reset. This isn't a destination, it's a decompression stop between the park and the afternoon.
When energy is right — probably early-to-mid afternoon — take the Keio Inokashira Line one stop to Shimokitazawa. Start around Bonus Track for food stalls and coffee, then wander the surrounding streets for vintage shops and cafés. Plan for 90 minutes to two hours; bail when the day tells you to. Easy train back to Ueno.

items.title: Kanda Myojin Shrine
items.description: Walk to Kanda Myojin Shrine. Quiet morning shrine near Akihabara.

items.title: Akihabara: Yodobashi Camera + arcades
items.description: Head into Akihabara for an hour of electronics stores and arcades. Yodobashi Camera works well for organized browsing and air conditioning. Carrier

items.title: JINS glasses pickup + pack
items.description: If not picked up earlier

items.title: Get to Narita
items.description:
8:35 AM — leave hotel
8:55 AM — arrive Keisei-Ueno Station
9:20 AM — Skyliner departs
10:11 AM — arrive Narita T1
10:15–10:30 AM — get to KE check-in
12:45 PM — wheels up

items.title: Fly to Busan
items.description:
Korean Air 130 @ 12:45. 
Confirmation: DPOMRS; 2 checked bags (maybe 3 w/ Wren)
Should have bassinet seat

items.title: Check into Airbnb
items.description:
3rd Floor, Domestic Terminal CTS. Reservation X-DTC10S1EAMS

Taxi (~22 min, ₩25,000–35,000 / ~$18–25)
Walk out of the international terminal to the taxi stand — orange/silver "regular" taxis are fine, no need for "international" deluxe taxis (which are 30–40% more for the same ride).

Show the driver this in Korean: 부산 수영구 민락수변로 49 (KCC 스위첸)

Cash or card both work, but have some won on hand. Most taxis accept T-money/credit cards now, but not all.

Worth knowing: Kakao T is the local rideshare app (Korea's Uber). If you set it up before you land, you can hail one from the airport, fix the destination in-app so there's no language friction, and pay through the app. It uses the same regular taxi fleet, same metered fare.

items.title: Gwangalli Beach evening walk
items.description: Take a relaxed walk along Gwangalli Beach — wide promenade, flat paths, and one of Busan’s best night views as Gwangan Bridge lights up after dark. Dinner along the beachfront; keep it easy. If energy is high, Millak Raw Fish Market is an option, but not required on night one.

items.title: Songdo Cloud Trails
items.description: Head west to Songdo in the morning. Start with the Songdo Cloud Trails — a glass-and-steel boardwalk extending out over the sea. Flat and easy even in a carrier, with great coastal views. Carrier.

items.title: Busan Air Cruise
items.description: Continue to the Busan Air Cruise and ride the crystal cabin across the bay. Views of the coastline, harbor, and Namhang Bridge from above.

items.title: Amnam Park stroll + Songdo Beach
items.description: On the far side, stroll briefly through Amnam Park — shaded paths, ocean air — then ride the cable car back. Or, optional short walk along Songdo Beach before returning to Gwangalli by early–mid afternoon.

items.title: Gamcheon Culture Village
items.description: Out early to Gamcheon Culture Village to beat heat and crowds. Hillside alleys, murals, and overlooks — famously nicknamed the “Machu Picchu of Busan.” Carrier only. Focus on the main viewpoints and a few side alleys; two hours is plenty. Carrier.

items.title: Jagalchi Fish Market lunch
items.description: Taxi or bus down to Jagalchi Fish Market for a seafood lunch — either upstairs with harbor views or at ground-level stalls.

items.title: BIFF Square street food
items.description: Walk over to BIFF Square afterward for Busan’s iconic street food, especially seed-filled hotteok.

items.title: Rest / buffer day
items.description: Gwangalli Beach in the morning, neighborhood cafés, and an easy walk to Millac The Market — a modern food hall with space to roam and sit. If weather disrupted an earlier day, this is where you recover it. Dinner nearby; Korean BBQ works well tonight.

items.title: Haeundae Blueline Park: Sky Capsule + Beach Train
items.description:
Head to Mipo for Haeundae Blueline Park. Ride the Sky Capsule from Mipo to Cheongsapo. The coastal rail line hugs the cliffs with nonstop sea views.
Carrier.

items.title: Daritdol Skywalk + Cheongsapo lunch
items.description:
At Cheongsapo, walk to the Daritdol Skywalk — a short glass platform over the water near the lighthouse. Lunch on grilled clams or seafood in the fishing village. 

Return via the Beach Train, riding the full coastal route back to Mipo. 

Optional short walk along Haeundae Beach before heading home

items.title: Haedong Yonggungsa Temple
items.description:
Early taxi east (~45–50 minutes) to Haedong Yonggungsa, aiming to arrive around 8am before tour buses. One of Korea’s rare seaside temples, built directly into the cliffs.

Descend the stone steps through bamboo to the temple complex — pagodas, statues, ocean spray, and dramatic views in every direction. Spend 1–1.5 hours, then head back before midday heat and crowds. Carrier.

items.title: Oryukdo Skywalk + Sunrise Park
items.description: Morning visit to Oryukdo Skywalk, perched above crashing waves where the East Sea meets the South Sea. Short glass walkway but dramatic setting. Walk up to Sunrise Park afterward for wider panoramic views. Carrier.

items.title: Farewell dinner Gwangalli beachfront
items.description: Farewell dinner on the beachfront with the bridge lit up.

items.title: Fly to Taipei
items.description:
China Air 0189 @ 12:15. 
Confirmation: ECETJ2; 2 checked bags per person

items.title: Check into Airbnb
items.description:
台北市信義區信義路四段415號
(Táiběi Shì Xìnyì Qū Xìnyì Lù Sì Duàn 415 Hào)

Take cab from airport

No. 415, Section 4, Xinyi Road, Tonghua Village, Xinyi District

items.title: Tonghua/Linjiang Night Market
items.description: Late afternoon walk to Tonghua/Linjiang Night Market — 5 minutes from your door. Grilled seafood, scallion pancakes, chicken cutlets, medicinal herb ribs soup, mango shaved ice. Wander, graze, get oriented. Home by 6:30.

items.title: Elephant Mountain hike
items.description: Before heat. Walk 20-30 mins, then steep 20-30 min up stairs with baby in carrier. Stop at Xiangshan Lookout if don't want to go whole way.

items.title: CKS Memorial Hall changing of guard
items.description: Head to Chiang Kai-Shek Memorial Hall — catch 10am changing of the guard (hourly). Liberty Square is massive, flat, stroller-friendly. Wren can roam.

items.title: Four Four South Village
items.description: Four Four South Village — literally across the street from your apartment. Restored military village with craft vendors, exhibitions, and a weekend market. Good coffee, shaded courtyards. Easy proximity finish. Home by 6.

items.title: Yongkang Street food crawl
items.description: Mango shaved ice at Smoothie House (peak season, non-negotiable). Tian Jin scallion pancakes. Tea/ceramic shops

items.title: Huashan 1914 Creative Park
items.description: Converted wine factory: pop-up exhibitions, cafés, courtyards

items.title: Raohe Night Market
items.description: Wren's one late night. Enter from Ciyou Temple end, eat toward MRT. Grilled seafood, fried chicken, herb ribs, stinky tofu, mango

items.title: Beitou Thermal Valley + Hot Springs Museum
items.description: MRT Red Line north to Beitou. Quick visit to Thermal Valley — steaming sulfur lake, dramatic and otherworldly. Then the Hot Springs Museum (free, gorgeous Japanese-era bathhouse, 20 min inside). Total Beitou time: about an hour.

items.title: Dihua Street + tea tasting
items.description: MRT back south to Shuanglian or Zhongshan station, walk to Dihua Street. This is old Taipei — Qing Dynasty shophouses, traditional Chinese medicine stores, dried goods, tea merchants. Do a proper tea tasting at one of the tea shops. Browse the craft and souvenir stores

items.title: Head to Jiufen
items.description: Klook driver picks you up at the apartment, to Jiufen by 9:30am before the tour bus crush. Hillside tea house village, narrow alleys, red lanterns, ocean views. Chest carrier for Wren — steep stairs, tight passages.

items.title: Jiufen tea house service
items.description: Duck into a traditional tea house for a proper Taiwanese tea service with mountain views.

items.title: Shifen sky lantern release + waterfall
items.description: Continue to Shifen — release a sky lantern together (write wishes, watch it float). Visit Shifen Waterfall (easy walk to viewing platforms). Watch the train run through the middle of the old street. Head back to Taipei by 3–3:30pm.

items.title: Maokong Gondola + tea tasting
items.description:
MRT to Taipei Zoo station (you're just using it as the gondola boarding point, not going into the zoo). Take the crystal-floor cabin up to Maokong — tea plantations, mountain views, cool air. At the top, do a proper tea tasting at one of the hillside tea houses, grab tea-flavored ice cream, let Wren crawl around the outdoor terraces. It's quieter up here than anywhere else you'll be all week. Ride back down by noon.

Toddler-friendly restaurant with play room

Maokong Ching Chuen Square
116, Taipei City, Wenshan District, Lane 38, Section 3, Zhinan Rd, 33之1號對面山坡

items.title: Da'an Forest Park
items.description: Head here in the afternoon for a playground, pond, shaded paths, flat stroller loops.

items.title: Fly to Chiang Mai
items.description:
Starlux JX751 @ 1:20 PM
Confirmation: FVBWXZ; 1 checked bag per person

items.title: Check into hotel
items.description:
Hotel will arrange ride

Proud Phu Fah 97/5 Moo 1, Mae Rim Samoeng Rd. (km. 18) T. Pongyang, Mae Rim, Chiang Mai 50180, Thailand

items.title: Queen Sirikit Botanical Garden Canopy Walk
items.description: Queen Sirikit Canopy Walk Arrive right when the botanical garden opens (~8:30 AM) to beat the heat and crowds. Rainforest canopy walkway, tropical greenhouses, stroller-friendly paths — expect about 2 hours total.

items.title: Pool / grounds at Proud Phu Fah
items.description: Head back to Proud Phu Fah by late morning and spend the rest of the day at the pool and grounds. August afternoons bring rain, so let the resort do the work — this is one of those days where doing less is doing more.

items.title: Elephant sanctuary day
items.description: Need to determine if we want ENP or Chiang Chill - Elephant Sanctuary Full-day pickup from Proud Phu Fah (~8-9 AM, return 3-4 PM). Ethical sanctuary — feeding, observing, walking alongside elephants in their habitat. Bring a carrier instead of the Doona — paths are muddy and uneven, especially in rainy season. Pack a change of clothes for Wren and expect standing time; choose an observation-style program which is easier with a toddler.

items.title: Check into Airbnb
items.description:
178 Loi Kroh Road
Tambon Chang Moi, Chang Wat Chiang Mai 50100

items.title: Transfer to Erawan Condo (Old City)
items.description: Check out Proud Phu Fah, transfer to Night Bazaar area

items.title: Old City temple circuit on foot
items.description: Afternoon temple circuit on foot in this order: Wat Phra Singh → Wat Chedi Luang → Wat Phan Tao, with an optional quick stop at Wat Chiang Man. Plan about 60–75 minutes total — the temples are clustered close together inside the moat. Wren rides in the Doona; dress modestly, shoulders and knees covered.

items.title: Night Bazaar street food
items.description: End the day exploring the Night Bazaar area on foot — it's right outside your door and the street food is excellent.

items.title: Doi Suthep + Wat Pha Lat
items.description:
Leave early (7:30–8 AM) for Wat Phra That Doi Suthep — cloud cover and rain build by late morning in August, so timing matters. Take the 306-step dragon staircase or the elevator for the Doona; city views are best in early light. On the way back down, stop at Wat Pha Lat — the quiet jungle temple hidden in the forest, one of the most atmospheric spots in Chiang Mai and an easy stroller-friendly pause.
Time the descent so you're heading into the Old City by around 11:15–11:30.

items.title: Khao Soi Khun Yai
items.description: Have your driver (or Grab) drop you at Khao Soi Khun Yai (Sri Poom 8 Alley, northeast corner inside the moat) for an early lunch. It opens at 10 and closes at 2, but regularly sells out well before then, so arriving before 11:30 matters — they don't take reservations and it's cash only, so bring small bills. Bowls run 40–70 THB depending on protein (chicken is the house favorite; beef also excellent). It's open-air, plastic-tables-in-a-lot, no AC

items.title: Tina aerial yoga at Satva
items.description: 4 PM Class. Go today so you can go again if you like it. Leave around 3:20, should take 20 minutes by tuktuk to get there.

items.title: Hotel massage + pool
items.description: If Tina doesn't go to aerial yoga, let's have people come to the hotel to give us massages

items.title: Bo Sang Umbrella Village
items.description: Watch artisans craft bamboo-and-paper umbrellas, then do the painting workshop. Expect it to be touristy and short (30–45 minutes)

items.title: Tina art class: Lai Thai pattern OR watercolors
items.description: Tina does a 2-hour Lai Thai pattern class at Chamai Art Workshop or watercolors at Three Owls Gallery while Eli takes Wren to a playground or pool. Tina takes home her artwork as a one-of-a-kind souvenir — better than anything you'd buy at the night market.

items.title: Cooking class with market visit
items.description: Cooking Class Morning class at a family-friendly cooking school that accommodates kids and dietary preferences — starts with a market visit to pick ingredients, then cook 3-4 dishes (pad thai, green curry, mango sticky rice, tom yum). No pork, easily accommodated.

items.title: Mobile Thai massage at Erawan
items.description: Thai massage for Eli and Tina — $5-8/hour, you'll never find prices like this again. Book a mobile massage to the Erawan so you don't have to schlep anywhere post-cooking class.

items.title: Carp Café (Saraphi)
items.description: Grab to Carp Café in Saraphi (~20 min from Old City). Get there when they open to beat crowds. Order drinks, buy a couple bags of fish food, and let Wren lose her mind watching hundreds of koi swarm at her feet from the sunken tables. The gardens are worth a wander too — bridges, waterfalls, tucked-away ponds. Don't plan to eat a real meal here; the food gets mediocre reviews across the board. Budget an hour, maybe 90 minutes, then Grab back.

items.title: Tina aerial yoga at Satva
items.description: Tina does an aerial yoga class at Satva Yoga Chiang Mai while you and Wren hold down the apartment — nap, pool, low-key daddy-daughter time. Afternoon option: Warorot Market (Kad Luang) for the real local market experience — dried fruits, spices, flowers, fabrics.

items.title: Sunday Walking Street (Ratchadamnoen)
items.description: Evening: hit the Sunday Walking Street on Ratchadamnoen Road if timing lines up — the most authentic market experience in Chiang Mai, far better than the Night Bazaar for crafts and street food.

items.title: Fly to Luang Prabang
items.description:
Lao Air QV636 @ 3:15. 
Confirmation: UCBKEU; 1 checked bag per person

items.title: Check into Airbnb
items.description:
Ban Wat Nong Unit4 House No 97 "Hom's Home"

The house is located in Ban Wat Nong next to Mekong Road, just enter the small side way in-between Saffron Café and Big Tree Café coming from the Mekong Road, our house is the 4st one on your right-hand side, called "Hom's Home". The name is marked above the entrance door. Coming from the airport or train station, taxi drivers would know where Big Tree Café and Saffron Cafe are located on Mekong Road

items.title: Grilled fish + sticky rice from night market
items.description: Grab grilled fish and sticky rice from the nearest street vendor or night market food stalls. Eat at home.

items.title: Mount Phousi sunrise climb
items.description: Mount Phousi early, ~7:30–8 AM before the heat. 328 steps, ~20 min up. 360° views over both rivers and the peninsula. Down by 8:30. Browse the morning market nearby for fruit and snacks.

items.title: Wat Xieng Thong
items.description: Walk out to Wat Xieng Thong at the tip of the peninsula — LP's most important temple, stunning gold-on-black mosaics.

items.title: Visit Pool Club
items.description:
La Pistoche swimming pool and bar for easy splash afternoons with Wren - 7 min tuktuk ride (~$2.50 to get in)

or

Luang Prabang View Hotel if you want a nicer infinity-pool experience. 15 mins away

items.title: Big Brother Mouse - first visit
items.description: Big Brother Mouse - first visit. English conversation with local students/monks

items.title: Kuang Si Waterfall + Bear Rescue Centre
items.description: Kuang Si Waterfall Depart 8 AM by tuk-tuk (~45 min, bumpy road). Bear Rescue Centre at the entrance, then the falls. Late August means spectacular water volume but pools may be too rough for swimming. Paths can be muddy — shoes with grip, rain poncho, Wren in carrier.

items.title: Laos Buffalo Dairy stop (optional)
items.description: Optional stop at Laos Buffalo Dairy on the way back (ice cream, feed buffalos — verify it's operating). Back by early afternoon.

items.title: Ock Pop Tok - Batik Dye Class
items.description:
Half day Batik + Indigo dye class booked for tina. Eli and Wren do the free guided tour, watch artisan demos, lunch at their Mekong-view restaurant. 

They are giving us a ride from their shop, need to be there at 8:30.

items.title: 2nd Visit Pool Club
items.description:
La Pistoche swimming pool and bar for easy splash afternoons with Wren - 7 min tuktuk ride (~$2.50 to get in)

or

Luang Prabang View Hotel if you want a nicer infinity-pool experience. 15 mins away

items.title: Big Brother Mouse - second visit
items.description: Try to arrive by 5 PM.

items.title: Temple street wander: Royal Palace, Wat Mai, Wat Sene, Wat Visounarat
items.description: Wander the temple streets — Royal Palace Museum, Wat Mai, Wat Sene, Wat Visounarat, all walkable. Or slow café morning (Saffron Coffee has a Mekong terrace, Joma has great pastries and AC). Morning market if you haven't properly explored it yet.

items.title: Mekong sunset boat ride (1 hour, ~$10-15)
items.description: Book same day near ferry pier. Back by 5:30

items.title: Tamarind cooking class
items.description: Cooking class + Massage Morning: Tamarind cooking class, 9 AM–3 PM. Market visit first, then cook at their countryside pavilion (~20 min from town). You do the cooking, Tina holds Wren in the open-air space. ~5 dishes: jeow (dips), mok pa (fish in banana leaf), stuffed lemongrass, sticky rice, purple sticky rice dessert. Eat your food for lunch

items.title: Back-to-back in-home massages
items.description: Take turns watching Wren while other person gets massage

items.title: Big Brother Mouse
items.description: Can go back to BBM if we want

items.title: Living Land Farm (optional)
items.description: Whatever you missed or want to revisit. Living Land Farm (hands-on organic rice farm, family-friendly). Or sunset boat if you didn't do it Day 5. Or nothing — LP rewards doing nothing.

items.title: Big Brother Mouse - final visit
items.description: Come back one last time if we want

items.title: Garavek Storytelling (optional)
items.description: Traditional Laos stories told in English from 630-730 PM. Maybe move later in week.

items.title: Fly to Hanoi
items.description:
Vietnam Airline VN 930 @4:05 
Confirmation: FSMJBI; 1 checked bag per person
~70 minute flight

items.title: Check into Airbnb
items.description:
3 Aliza
68A Phố Từ Hoa
Quảng An, Tây Hồ, Hà Nội3

2 PM Checkin
items.address:
3 Aliza
68A Phố Từ Hoa
Quảng An, Tây Hồ, Hà Nội3

items.title: Old Quarter wander + Hoan Kiem Lake
items.description: Grab to the Old Quarter. Wander the 36 streets — each named for the trade historically practiced there. Stroller-navigable but chaotic, so baby carrier might be easier for the narrow sidewalks. Hoan Kiem Lake: walk around the lake, cross the red Huc Bridge to Ngoc Son Temple on the island. Calm, shady, photogenic.

items.title: Egg coffee at Giang Cafe (the original)
items.description: The original place to get it. Can go elsewhere if line too long.

items.title: Street Food Lunch
items.description: Street food lunch — bun cha (find a chicken version), pho, banh mi.

items.title: Train Street 🚂
items.description: Head to Train Street. Weekend schedule has daytime passes around 9 AM, 11:20 AM, 3:20 PM. The Old Quarter section requires a café owner to escort you in — contact Railway Cafe, Cafe Ga Dong Duong, or Track 5 Cafe in advance via Instagram or WhatsApp. Or hit the Lê Duẩn section near the main railway station, which is fully open with no barriers. Arrive 30 min before a scheduled pass, grab a café seat, order egg coffee, watch the train squeeze through as a family. Budget about an hour total. Afterward, walk to Hoan Kiem area for an early lunch or revisit anything you missed Saturday.

items.title: Tran Quoc Pagoda + West Lake walk
items.description: West Lake walk, Tran Quoc Pagoda — one of Hanoi's oldest temples, easy to access, on a small peninsula.

items.title: In-home massage at Airbnb
items.description: Have someone come give massages during afternoon/evening

items.title: Open day - flex
items.description: No fixed plan. Options depending on energy and mood: Vietnamese Women's Museum (well-curated, air-conditioned, stroller-friendly). Cooking class — Hanoi has excellent ones, most are half-day morning sessions. Second Old Quarter run if you felt rushed on Saturday. Or just be in Tay Ho — wander, eat, let Wren set the pace. The neighborhood rewards aimless exploration.

items.title: In-home massage at Airbnb
items.description: Have them come to Airbnb

items.title: Pack up for Cruise + Ninh Binh
items.description: Figure out what we can leave at Airbnb

items.title: Pickup for Cruise
items.description: We need to hire our own driver. Need to confirm arrival time and book.

items.title: Arrive Tuan Chau Harbor + tender to Sena
items.description: No. 25 Tuan Chau Marina Terminal 2. Welcome drink, briefing, cabin check-in

items.title: Lunch sailing through Lan Ha Bay
Dark and Bright Cave by bamboo boat
items.description: Plus swimming in Lan Ha Bay

items.title: Sunset deck party + spring rolls cooking class
Viet Hai Village by day boat
items.description: Bike or tram rides, traditional houses, scenic trails

items.title: Pristine bay: swim, sunbathe, kayak
items.description: From day boat

items.title: Kayaking & swimming at Tra Bau
items.description: Final morning

items.title: Ride to Tam Coc Retreat - Ninh Binh
items.description:
Đội 2 thôn Hải Nham, Ninh Hải, Nam Hoa Lư, Ninh Bình 430000, Vietnam

~3.5-4 hours. Wren naps in car

Still need to hire driver

items.title: Thung Nham Bird Garden (game-time call)
items.description: Game-time decision around 3:30 PM: If on track to arrive by 4:15–4:30 PM, driver goes directly to Thung Nham Bird Garden (skip hotel check-in). Birds return to roost after 4:30 PM, peak spectacle ~5:00–5:30 PM. Tina's moment — bring camera with zoom. Entry 150,000 VND/adult, free under 1 meter. ~5:30 PM back in the car, 15 min to Tam Coc Retreat. Check in, easy dinner. If running late or everyone is fried, driver goes straight to Tam Coc Retreat. Check in, decompress, easy dinner. Thung Nham moves to Sep 11 afternoon (guaranteed slot).

items.title: Check in Tam Coc Retreat
items.description:
Đội 2 thôn Hải Nham

Family Villa Room

items.title: Mua Cave climb
items.description: (~7:00 AM): Mua Cave. Get there before the heat and tour groups. 500 steps up to the dragon statue overlook — panoramic view over Tam Coc valley, karst peaks, and river winding through rice paddies. One of the best viewpoints in northern Vietnam. Wren in the carrier. Bring water. In and out before it gets crowded.

items.title: Tam Coc boat ride
items.description: Straight to Tam Coc boat ride (5 minutes from Mua Cave). Narrower waterway than Trang An, rice paddies on both sides, more intimate. Morning light is best. Shorter loop — 60–75 minutes is plenty.

items.title: Lunch + nap + pool at Tam Coc Retreat
items.description: Back to Tam Coc Retreat. Lunch. Wren naps. Pool. Slow afternoon — no schedule, no driving.

items.title: Thung Nham Bird Garden (primary or revisit)
items.description: If you went on Sep 10 evening, this is a relaxed second visit with different light. If you didn't make it Sep 10, this is the primary visit — unhurried, well-timed for peak roosting.

items.title: Driver picks up, head to Cuc Phuong (~45 min)
items.description: Relaxed morning at Tam Coc Retreat first

items.title: Endangered Primate Rescue Center (EPRC)
items.description: ~11:15 AM: Endangered Primate Rescue Center (EPRC). Expert tour, ~1–1.5 hours. Langurs, gibbons, lorises — one of the best conservation programs in Southeast Asia. Turtle Conservation Center nearby.

items.title: Lunch at Cuc Phuong HQ
items.description: Lunch at park headquarters.

items.title: Cuc Phuong Bear Sanctuary
items.description: Bear Sanctuary. Bears just fed at 1 PM, they'll be active. Budget about 45 minutes to an hour.

items.title: Drive Cuc Phuong to Hanoi
items.description: Should be a three hour drive

items.title: Decompress, laundry, groceries, pack
items.description: Decompress, laundry, groceries. Final neighborhood dinner. Pack for Hoi An.

items.title: Fly to Hoi An
items.description:
Vietnam Airline VN 171 @11:45 
Confirmation: DZQJSG; 1 checked bag per person
~90 minute flight

items.title: Check into Airbnb
items.description:
37, Lý Nam Đế, Phường Sơn Phong, Thành phố Hội An, Quảng Nam 560000

2 PM Checkin

items.title: Settle in, maybe check out a tailor
items.description: Grocery run for breakfast staples since you have a kitchen. Grab an easy dinner nearby, get Wren down. Maybe a short evening walk to get your bearings and mentally note which tailor shops to hit tomorrow. No agenda — you're coming off 9 intense days in Hanoi. Just land.

items.title: Go to A Dong for measurements
items.description:
Ask about canvassed vs. fused construction, fabric sourcing, and whether they can do 3 fittings across your timeline. The whole circuit takes about 2 hours if you're thorough.

Bring examples (pictures) or suits, dresses, and outfits we want made. place orders — Eli's suit, Tina's silk pajamas, Tina's sandals (sandal shops are separate, scope those during the walk). Get measured, choose fabrics and leather, confirm fitting schedule for Day 4. Bring reference photos for the suit and a dress shirt to wear during measurements. Pay no more than 50% deposit upfront.

items.title: Old town wander at Wren's pace
items.description: First real wander through the old town at Wren's pace. Riverside, market, lantern streets. Evening dinner in the Ancient Town.

items.title: Cooking class with market tour
items.description: Cooking class. Most start with a market tour to pick ingredients — Hoi An's market is most interesting early. Cook 3–4 dishes, confirm no pork. Wren in high chair. Red Bridge, Thuan Tinh Island, and Morning Glory's school are solid options. Classes typically start around 8:30–9 AM and run about 3 hours.

items.title: Cam Thanh Coconut Forest basket boats
items.description: Cam Thanh Coconut Forest basket boats, about 10 minutes from the old town. Round basket boats through coconut palm-lined waterways — the guides spin the boats and it's genuinely fun. Wren will probably love the motion and the water. Budget 1–2 hours. Ask your hotel for a small, quiet operator — avoid the loud docks near the big parking lot. The best experience is a slow, peaceful paddle through the palms.

items.title: Thanh Ha Pottery Village workshop
items.description: Thanh Ha Pottery Village, about 3 km west of the old town. Hands-on workshop — shape clay on a wheel, decorate your pieces. Family-friendly, Wren can play with clay alongside you. Entry is 35,000 VND (~$1.50), workshops are a small additional fee. Budget about 2 hours. Unlike lanterns, you're not carrying something fragile for 3 more months. The village is peaceful, uncrowded, and a nice change of pace from the old town.

items.title: First tailor fittings
items.description: Afternoon: first round of fittings. Suit (check cut and drape), pajamas (check fit), sandals (should be close to done — check strap comfort and sole). Be honest about what doesn't look right.

items.title: An Bang Beach morning
items.description: An Bang Beach. Head toward the Soul Kitchen / Shore Club section for a calmer, cleaner stretch. Beach chairs and umbrellas are free from beachfront restaurants if you order food. Let Wren dig in the sand, splash in the shallows. Relaxed morning — not a full beach day, head back around lunchtime.

items.title: Second tailor fittings
items.description: Afternoon: second round of fittings. Sandals and pajamas should be done or very close — try everything on carefully. Suit check: sit down, raise your arms, button and unbutton. If it needs one more tweak, the tailor has overnight to make adjustments for morning pickup tomorrow. Pay balances on sandals and pajamas once satisfied.

items.title: Suit Pickup
items.description: Morning: pick up the suit (final). Try it on one last time. Pay balance once satisfied.

items.title: Old town deep dive: 5 heritage sites
items.description: Late morning/afternoon: the parts of old town you haven't explored yet. Japanese Covered Bridge, Phuc Kien Assembly Hall, Tan Ky Old House, Central Market. Buy a 120,000 VND old town ticket covering 5 heritage sites.

items.title: Lantern-lit riverside stroll + boat ride
items.description: Lantern-lit riverside stroll along the Thu Bon River. Maybe a short boat ride. Street food dinner. By day 6 you know the good banh mi spot, the cafe with the best egg coffee, the shortcut through the back streets. Last night in Hoi An — soak it in.

items.title: Fly to Siem Reap
items.description:
Air Cambodie K6 841@4:35 
Confirmation: FYDPCI; 1 checked bag per person
~2 hour flight

items.title: Check into Bliss Ankgor
items.description:
Bliss Ankgor

items.title: Banteay Srei + Banteay Samré
items.description: Banteay Srei + Banteay Samré 45-minute drive to Banteay Srei — the finest carved detail work in all of Angkor, pink sandstone, far fewer people than the main complex. Banteay Samré is a short detour on the way back, beautiful and nearly empty — game-time call based on energy.

items.title: Angkor Wat sunrise + Ta Prohm + Bayon
items.description: 4:30am pickup. Sunrise at Angkor Wat, then straight to Ta Prohm while it's still empty — the tree roots swallowing the stone walls hit different without crowds. Bayon and Angkor Thom after, with optional Terrace of Elephants if Wren's holding up. Done by 11:30am

items.title: Pool recovery afternoon
items.description: Vibe out by the pool, we could also move massages to this day if we want.

items.title: Countryside cooking class with Ben
items.description: Countryside Cooking Class with Ben Morning pickup, 30 minutes out to a non-touristy local market where Ben walks you through the stalls, then to his countryside kitchen to cook a 3-course Khmer meal. $24/person, 20% supports local orphans.

items.title: Alternate massages at Bliss
items.description:
One of the massages we have included in our booking. 

Swedish Massage 60mn 
Aromatherapy Khmer Body Massage 60mn

items.title: Samatoa Lotus Silk Weaving
items.description:
Plan for 10 AM Pickup (Biolab Café on Wat Bo Road), 10:30 class (90 minutes) with lunch after. Boat tour after.

Go behind the scenes at Lotus Silk Farm and discover how Cambodia’s rare lotus silk is transformed from flower stem to finished textile. During this hands-on artisan experience, you'll try traditional crafts including lotus fiber spinning, bracelet making, and lotus paper creation, then enjoy a farm-fresh Khmer lunch featuring lotus-inspired dishes. If time permits, cap off the visit with a peaceful boat ride through the surrounding lotus fields for a closer look at this unique agricultural landscape.

items.title: Artisans Angkor workshop
items.description: Morning walk-through of the Artisans Angkor workshop — watch silk weaving and stone carving in action, free to enter, short and low-effort with Wren.

~1.5 hour flight

items.title: Land MLE, seaplane to Maamigili, boat to Dhigurah
items.description:
Clear immigration and collect bags, then walk to the seaplane terminal — Trans Maldivian Airways or Manta Air operates the Maamigili route. Typical wait after international landing is 1.5–3 hours. Pack a change of clothes for Wren in your day bag — the seaplane terminal isn't air-conditioned like the international terminal. Flight is ~25 minutes to the Maamigili water platform, then a short speedboat (~10 min) to Dhigurah.

Hotel is arranging flights

items.title: Dhigurah bikini beach + Hermit's dinner
items.description: At the property by late afternoon. Welcome drinks, crib setup, walk Wren on the bikini beach at the south end of the village for her first Indian Ocean moment. Dinner at Hermit's — in-house guests get priority and it saves the village walk on a tired travel day.

items.title: Whale shark trip #1 (group)
items.description:
Whale shark group trip #1, all three of you. First attempt. Book with an operator that explicitly accepts toddlers — not all do; standard liability cutoff at some Dhigurah ops is 5 or 6 years old. TGI Maldives and smaller dhoni-based outfits tend to be more flexible than the big speedboat operations. Ask Bliss to specify "child age 16 months will be on board, parents taking turns in water" when booking. Morning departure 8:00–9:00 AM, return by 12:30–1:00 PM. ~$80–120/person; Wren rides free or token charge depending on operator. Pack: child life jacket (confirm boat supplies a toddler-sized one or bring/buy your own), shaded canopy on the boat, water/snacks for Wren, sun shirt and hat. Mechanic: dive guide spots the shark, one parent slips in, the other stays on the boat with Wren. Most morning trips get 2–4 sighting attempts, so each of you should get at least one in-water moment. 

Pre-trip: email MWSRP at info@maldiveswhalesharkresearch.org once dates are firm to ask if their citizen science boat is running this week and whether they accept toddlers — if both yes, swap it in. Same encounters, you're on a research boat doing photo-ID, data goes into the global Wildbook for Whale Sharks.

items.title: Afternoon and Evening
items.description: Back by lunch — Hermit's or walk into the village (Hawwa's or Symphony are the reliable local cafes). Afternoon nap. Evening stroller walk along the long beach toward the south sandbank for sunset.

items.title: Recovery and family day
items.description: Nothing booked. Morning at the Bliss pool — small but it's there, which puts Bliss ahead of most Dhigurah guesthouses. Late morning walk into the village proper (Bliss is on the south end, village core is ~10 minutes north on foot). The MWSRP office is here if they're willing to host a short visit — worth asking when you email. Lunch at a local cafe. Afternoon: Wren naps, one of you snorkels the house reef directly off the bikini beach, the other reads. The Dhigurah house reef is unspectacular by Maldivian standards but you'll see reef fish and occasionally turtles. Save Tina's serious snorkeling energy for the excursions. Sunset sandbank walk — the south end extends into a kilometer-long natural sandbank when tides are right, one of the most photogenic stretches of beach in the country. Dinner at Hermit's.

items.title: Private boat charter (whale sharks)
items.description: Private boat charter, all three of you. Splurge day. Half-day private dhoni or speedboat through Bliss or directly with Sea Senses or Dhigurah Dive Center, ~$400–700 depending on boat type and duration. Book in advance — private charters get snapped up by honeymooners. Departure 8:30 AM to catch morning whale shark activity before wind picks up. Advantage over the group trip: no waiting for 12 other tourists to gear up, captain heads directly to the SAMPA hotspots, you can return to Dhigurah on 15 minutes' notice if Wren melts down. Same in-water rotation as Day 3. Confirm with the captain before booking: toddler-sized life jacket, shaded canopy, snacks/water on board

items.title: Dinner in the village
items.description: Head into the village for dinner to mix it up

items.title: Sandbank picnic excursion
items.description: Half-day boat trip to a remote uninhabited sandbank for a guided picnic and snorkel — ~$50–100/person through Bliss. Pure white sand, ankle-deep water in a 360-degree ring around it, snorkeling the drop-off if you want it, lunch served on the sand. Wren will love this. Photos that will outlast everything else from this leg. Back to Bliss by mid-afternoon. If weather's bad or everyone's tired, swap for an in-village turtle reef snorkel from the beach (turtles reliably present at the southern reef edge, free, no boat needed) and call it a beach day. Built in deliberately as your slack day.

items.title: Whale shark #3 OR full beach day
items.description: Game-day decision. If Days 3 and 5 produced strong sightings, skip this — you've had your moment. If the first two were thin, this is your insurance attempt. Same logistics as Day 3: ~8 AM departure, back by 1 PM, $80–120/person, all three of you. If you skip: long morning at the pool, late breakfast, walk the full length of the long beach (~3 km, rare in the Maldives), one last lunch at Hermit's. Afternoon packing for the early departure.

items.title: Pack travel kit for tomorrow
items.description: Passports, Wren's flight snacks, charged phones, formula. Pre-arrange 5 AM speedboat 48 hours out

items.title: Speedboat Dhigurah to Maamigili
items.description: As of the now the plan is to take the 5 AM speedboat. This will be updated once we determine where we are going
